questions.q: Who directed the 1972 Malayalam film Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram (1972) was the directorial debut of Adoor Gopalakrishnan.

questions.q: In which year was the Malayalam film Swayamvaram released?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram was released in 1972.

questions.q: In which Indian language was the film Swayamvaram made?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram is a Malayalam-language film set in Kerala.

questions.q: The film Swayamvaram was the feature-film debut of which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram marked Adoor Gopalakrishnan's debut as a feature-film director.

questions.q: Which actress played the female lead in the 1972 film Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Sharada played the female lead, Sita, in Swayamvaram.

questions.q: Which veteran Malayalam actor played the male lead in Swayamvaram (1972)?
questions.explanation: Madhu played the male lead, Vishwam, in Swayamvaram.

questions.q: The title of the film Swayamvaram is commonly translated into English as which phrase?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram is usually translated as 'One's Own Choice'.

questions.q: Swayamvaram (1972) is widely credited with launching which movement in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram is widely cited as the film that launched the Malayalam new wave.

questions.q: Swayamvaram won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film for which year's films?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film for 1972.

questions.q: The style of cinema that Swayamvaram (1972) championed is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram marked a decisive break toward realist, art-house cinema in Malayalam.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards did the film Swayamvaram (1972) win in total?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram won four National Film Awards: Best Feature Film, Best Direction, Best Actress and Best Cinematography.

questions.q: Which actress won the National Film Award for Best Actress for her performance in Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Sharada won the National Film Award for Best Actress for Swayamvaram.

questions.q: What are the names of the central couple whose story drives the plot of Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram follows a young couple named Vishwam and Sita.

questions.q: What is the central premise of the story in Swayamvaram (1972)?
options: A king choosing an heir to his throne
A young couple who defy convention and struggle to build a life together
A detective solving a village murder
A soldier returning home after a war
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram depicts a young couple who reject social convention and face hardship trying to make a life on their own terms.

questions.q: Which cinematographer shot Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Swayamvaram (1972)?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram was photographed by Mankada Ravi Varma, a long-time collaborator of Adoor Gopalakrishnan.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan was a co-founder of which film society or cooperative associated with Swayamvaram's production milieu?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan co-founded the Chitralekha Film Cooperative, central to the rise of new-wave Malayalam cinema.

questions.q: Before turning to direction, Adoor Gopalakrishnan trained in film at which Indian institution?
options: Film and Television Institute of India, Pune
Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute, Kolkata
L. V. Prasad Film Academy, Chennai
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan studied at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune before making Swayamvaram.

questions.q: Which of these films is also a work directed by Adoor Gopalakrishnan, the maker of Swayamvaram?
questions.explanation: Elippathayam (1981) is another acclaimed film directed by Adoor Gopalakrishnan; Chemmeen, Nirmalyam and Uttarayanam were directed by others.

questions.q: In Swayamvaram, the male lead Vishwam hopes to make a living through which occupation after the couple leave their families?
questions.explanation: In Swayamvaram, Vishwam is an aspiring writer who struggles to support the couple after they elope and leave their families.

questions.q: Swayamvaram (1972) is set primarily in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram is a Malayalam film set in Kerala.

questions.q: Madhu, the male lead of Swayamvaram, was already an established star in which industry by 1972?
questions.explanation: Madhu was a leading Malayalam-cinema actor when he starred in Swayamvaram.

questions.q: The National Film Award for Best Feature Film, which Swayamvaram won, is associated with which medal?
questions.explanation: Best Feature Film carries the Swarna Kamal (Golden Lotus), which Swayamvaram received for 1972.

questions.q: Swayamvaram (1972) was selected as the Best Feature Film at the 20th edition of which Indian awards?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram won Best Feature Film at the 20th National Film Awards, honouring films of 1972.

questions.q: Besides Best Feature Film and Best Actress, Swayamvaram also won the National Film Award for Best Direction for which person?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan won the National Film Award for Best Direction for Swayamvaram.

questions.q: Swayamvaram's screenplay is credited to Adoor Gopalakrishnan along with which co-writer who was also a Chitralekha collaborator?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram was co-written by Adoor Gopalakrishnan and K. P. Kumaran.

questions.q: The fourth National Film Award won by Swayamvaram (1972) was in which technical category?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram's four National Film Awards included Best Cinematography, won by Mankada Ravi Varma.

questions.q: How does the narrative of Swayamvaram chiefly portray the couple's life after they elope?
options: As a comic series of misadventures with a happy ending
As a triumphant rise to wealth and fame
As a steadily worsening economic struggle and disillusionment
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram unflinchingly traces the couple's descent into poverty and hardship rather than offering a romantic resolution.

questions.q: Swayamvaram is frequently grouped with which broader 1970s movement of Indian art cinema funded partly through institutions like the FFC?
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram is a landmark of Indian parallel cinema, the New Indian Cinema movement of the 1970s.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan, director of Swayamvaram, was later honoured with which of India's highest film awards for lifetime achievement?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest honour in cinema, in 2004.

questions.q: In the closing of Swayamvaram, after hardship befalls the couple, the film is noted for ending on what kind of note?
options: An ambiguous, open-ended image of the surviving partner facing an uncertain future
A courtroom verdict in the couple's favour
A musical reunion of both families
questions.explanation: Swayamvaram closes on a stark, open-ended note, with Sita left to face an uncertain future after Vishwam's death.
